reviews.review_msg: Martin is the best prof ive ever had at beedie. I thought id hate this class, but Martin's teaching made this my favourite class all semester. I really enjoyed lectures and tutorials. Grading was fair, although beware of a high curve (Around 79 in my section). 79 is so so easy to get though so don't be scared of that number.

Martin is an absolute goat. Engaging lectures that are participation focused with in class activities. Plans out very engaging tutorials and is very hands on. Grade is dictated by a final group project + presentation, a mid term exam, a few small written assignments as well as weekly readiness quizzes and discussion posts.

The course itself is relatively easy. Participation is basically mandatory in lectures and tutorials. Group projects were worth 30%. Martin is a new prof, so I do believe he deserves some grace, however I felt the structure of the course was a bit different from other BUS requirements, not necessarily in a good/bad way, just different in general.
Great professor! Class is engaging and very much enjoyable. Weekly mini assignments but very manageable.
Best prof I've had at SFU, lectures and tutorials were very fun, this class was a blast.
this guy is a straight g. clear lecturer and a W mans. best prof at sfu on GOD
He is a new Prof. Took his first class on Monday, pretty a good instructor so far at Beedie
